---
title: "EFM3D and EVL"
phase: thesis
audience: public
status: current
owner: jan
format: html
---

## EFM3D and EVL: Egocentric 3D State Substrate {#efm3d}

**Primary source.** [EFM3D: A Foundation Model for Egocentric 3D Perception](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.10224) [@EFM3D-straub2024].

**Local source.** [`main.tex`](../../literature/tex-src/arXiv-EFM3D/main.tex), [`method.tex`](../../literature/tex-src/arXiv-EFM3D/method.tex), [`experiments.tex`](../../literature/tex-src/arXiv-EFM3D/experiments.tex), and [`supplemental_text.tex`](../../literature/tex-src/arXiv-EFM3D/supplemental_text.tex).

**Related ARIA-NBV pages.** [Project Aria](project_aria.qmd), [ASE dataset](../ase_dataset.qmd), [VIN implementation](../impl/vin_nbv.qmd), and [VIN feature proposals](../impl/vin_v2_feature_proposals.qmd).

### Core contribution

{{< gls egocentric-foundation-model-3d >}} supplies the actor-visible 3D representation that ARIA-NBV builds on. Its {{< gls egocentric-voxel-lifting >}} architecture lifts frozen 2D image features from posed, calibrated {{< gls project-aria >}} streams into a local gravity-aligned voxel grid and trains heads for 3D surface regression and {{< gls oriented-bounding-box >}} detection [@EFM3D-straub2024].

For ARIA-NBV, EFM3D is not a planner. It is the scene-state backbone that turns egocentric snippets, poses, calibration, and semi-dense points into occupancy, object, and evidence tensors suitable for candidate scoring.

### Verified paper signals

| signal | source-backed detail | ARIA-NBV relevance |
|---|---|---|
| Egocentric inputs | EVL consumes synchronized RGB/SLAM camera snippets, camera calibration, poses, and semi-dense point evidence. | Defines the actor-visible evidence contract for scoring candidate views. |
| Voxel lifting | Frozen DINOv2.5 features are lifted into a local gravity-aligned voxel grid anchored to the snippet pose. | Provides a fixed local scene field for VIN-style scoring, with known local-extent limits. |
| Geometry priors | Semi-dense point and free-space masks are injected into the voxel representation. | Gives interpretable known-surface, known-free, and observation-support channels for NBV diagnostics. |
| Surface head | The surface-regression head predicts voxel occupancy/surface evidence. | Useful as a compact reconstruction proxy for scene context. |
| OBB head | The detection head predicts centerness, 7-DoF boxes, class scores, and sparse post-processed OBBs. | Supports observed/predicted target selection without exposing GT boxes to the actor. |
| Dataset scale | The paper trains/evaluates on {{< gls aria-synthetic-environments >}} and Project Aria-related real datasets, with ASE providing GT meshes/annotations for supervised tasks. | Confirms the substrate for oracle labels and observed-state realism. |

### ARIA-NBV adoption

- **Core substrate:** use EVL/EFM3D outputs as frozen scene evidence for one-step RRI scoring, rollout state summaries, and target-conditioned candidate ranking.
- **Target contract:** use predicted or observed {{< gls oriented-bounding-box >}} support as actor-visible target input; keep GT meshes and GT target crops for offline labels/evaluation only.
- **Diagnostics:** preserve frame lineage, voxel extent, camera stream identity, calibration, and {{< gls machine-perception-services >}} / ASE point-source metadata in offline stores.

The compact state contract for ARIA-NBV is:

$$
s_t =
\left(
  P_t^{\mathrm{semi}},
  V_t^{\mathrm{EVL}},
  O_t^{\mathrm{pred}},
  \mathcal{Q}_t,
  h_t
\right),
$$

where $P_t^{\mathrm{semi}}$ is the semi-dense reconstruction proxy, $V_t^{\mathrm{EVL}}$ is the local voxel/evidence field, $O_t^{\mathrm{pred}}$ are actor-visible detections or target hypotheses, $\mathcal{Q}_t$ is the finite candidate set, and $h_t$ is rollout history.

### Do not adopt

- Do not treat EVL predictions as ground truth; they are actor-visible evidence.
- Do not ignore the fixed local voxel extent. Candidates can be partially out of bounds even when valid in the scene.
- Do not mix offline GT meshes/OBBs into target selection or scorer inputs for the main protocol.
- Do not keep implementation debug dictionaries in this literature page; feature-selection details belong in [VIN feature proposals](../impl/vin_v2_feature_proposals.qmd).

### Open risks / caveats

- Local voxel anchoring and coordinate-frame conventions can silently corrupt projection features and RRI labels.
- Dynamic objects, reflections, distant surfaces, and incomplete semi-dense support can weaken EVL evidence.
- EVL features should be logged with explicit config/checkpoint/source hashes because rollout/Q datasets will otherwise mix incomparable backbone states.
